key_insight: Graphlet-MP introduces a precomputed graphlet-histogram representation database that captures local crystal geometry at the level of sites, pairs, and angle triplets for data-efficient materials prediction.
engineering_relevance: This gives materials engineers an interpretable, structure-only feature space that can be applied even when experimental data are scarce and end-to-end deep learning is impractical.
method: Extract screened Voronoi-based graphlets from CIF crystal structures.
Build 79 histogram distributions across three orders: atomic sites, bonded pairs, and bond-angle triplets.
Provide an Earth Mover's Distance metric plus open-source code to generate and extend the database.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a full database covering 149,082 inorganic crystals from the Materials Project, with precomputed representations available for immediate use.
watch_item: The representation depends on Voronoi screening and CIF quality, so results may be sensitive to preprocessing choices and structural data fidelity.
paper_title: Graphlet Histogram Representation Database of Inorganic Crystals
